infobox.key: president of the Republic of Slovenia
infobox.value: ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Predsednik Republike Slovenije*), is the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") of [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"). The office was established on 23 December 1991 when the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(Slovenia) "National Assembly (Slovenia)") passed a new [constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_Slovenia "Constitution of Slovenia") after the separation of Slovenia from the [Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Federal_Republic_of_Yugoslavia "Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia"). Five individuals have occupied the post of president of the Republic since Slovenia achieved its independence. The incumbent is [Nataša Pirc Musar](/wiki/Nata%C5%A1a_Pirc_Musar "Nataša Pirc Musar"), Slovenia's first female president. The president's official workplace is the [Presidential Palace](/wiki/Government_Building_and_President%27s_Office "Government Building and President's Office") in [Ljubljana](/wiki/Ljubljana "Ljubljana").

The **president of Slovenia**, officially the **president of the Republic of Slovenia** ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Predsednik Republike Slovenije*), is the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") of [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"). The office was established on 23 December 1991 when the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(Slovenia) "National Assembly (Slovenia)") passed a new [constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_Slovenia "Constitution of Slovenia") after the separation of Slovenia from the [Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Socialist_Federal_Republic_of_Yugoslavia "Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia"). Five individuals have occupied the post of president of the Republic since Slovenia achieved its independence. The incumbent is [Nataša Pirc Musar](/wiki/Nata%C5%A1a_Pirc_Musar "Nataša Pirc Musar"), Slovenia's first female president. The president's official workplace is the [Presidential Palace](/wiki/Government_Building_and_President%27s_Office "Government Building and President's Office") in [Ljubljana](/wiki/Ljubljana "Ljubljana").

## Election and term of office

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=President_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Election and term of office")]

The president is directly elected by universal adult suffrage for a term of five years.[[2]](#cite_note-rtvinfo-2) Any Slovenian citizen of legal age (18 or more) may run for President, but may only hold office for two terms.[[2]](#cite_note-rtvinfo-2)

The president has no legally guaranteed [immunity](/wiki/Legal_immunity "Legal immunity") and may be [impeached](/wiki/Impeached "Impeached") during their time in office.

## Duties and functions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=President_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Duties and functions")]

The president of the Republic is the highest representative of the Slovenian state, belonging neither to the [legislative](/wiki/Parliament_of_Slovenia "Parliament of Slovenia"), [executive](/wiki/Government_of_Slovenia "Government of Slovenia"), [judicial](/wiki/Judiciary_of_Slovenia "Judiciary of Slovenia"), nor [constitutive](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_Slovenia "Constitutional Court of Slovenia") branches of state power. The president instead occupies a completely separate but coequal moderating branch by virtue of Article 102 of the Constitution.[[3]](#cite_note-3) To this end, the president represents Slovenia abroad, fosters national unity at home, and exercises supreme command and control over the Armed Forces of Slovenia as commander-in-chief. In the performance of these functions, the president upholds the rule of law and, through their ongoing [mediation](/wiki/Mediation "Mediation") and [arbitration](/wiki/Arbitration "Arbitration"), guarantees the continuity and orderly operation of Slovenia's democratic institutions.[[4]](#cite_note-4) This stabilising and integrative role is in keeping with the president's solemn oath taken at inauguration to "...uphold the constitutional order,…act according to my conscience and…do all in my power for the good of Slovenia".[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The [Constitution of Slovenia](/wiki/Constitution_of_Slovenia "Constitution of Slovenia") provides for a [parliamentary system of government](/wiki/Parliamentary_system_of_government "Parliamentary system of government"), whereby the presidency is a predominantly ceremonial institution with limited political power.[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6) Indeed, unlike in most parliamentary republics, the president is not even the *nominal* chief executive. Rather, the executive authority of Slovenia is explicitly vested in the Government with the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Slovenia "Prime Minister of Slovenia") as its head.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) Nevertheless, both the National Assembly and the Government are obliged as a matter of law and by the terms of their own standing orders to keep the president fully informed on matters of state, be they foreign or domestic.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) Moreover, the functions conferred on the president by the Constitution and subordinate [statute law](/wiki/Statute "Statute") require neither the [countersignature](/wiki/Countersignature "Countersignature") of the prime minister nor the [advice](/wiki/Advice_(constitutional_law) "Advice (constitutional law)") of the Government to be effective.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[a]](#cite_note-13) Thus, the powers and duties at the president's disposal are generally exercised either in their complete discretion, after consultation with or on the proposal of the Government (with the right to refuse a proposal and to act contrary to any advice tendered), or in accordance with constitutional restrictions in the case of certain ministerial functions.[[13]](#cite_note-14)

### Legislative functions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=President_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Legislative functions")]

Convene regular sessions of the National Assembly
:   The president of the Republic has the power to [call](/wiki/Writ_of_election "Writ of election") both [general](/wiki/General_election "General election") and [by-elections](/wiki/By-election "By-election") to the [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_of_Slovenia "National Assembly of Slovenia") and to convene its first [regular session](/wiki/Legislative_session "Legislative session") following a general election, provided [general elections](/wiki/Elections_in_Slovenia "Elections in Slovenia") must be called no later than fifteen days before the expiry of four years from the date of the first session of the outgoing National Assembly. Likewise, if the president dissolves the National Assembly before the expiration of its mandate and calls for snap elections, a new National Assembly must be elected no later than two months after the dissolution of the previous one. The term of the old National Assembly otherwise comes to an end by default upon the first session of a new National Assembly, which must be convoked by the president no later than twenty days after a general election.[[14]](#cite_note-15)[[15]](#cite_note-16)[[16]](#cite_note-17)

Convene extraordinary sessions of the National Assembly
:   The president of the Republic may convene an [extraordinary session](/wiki/Special_session "Special session") of the National Assembly.[[17]](#cite_note-18) The calling together of an extraordinary session of the National Assembly concurrently reconvenes the [National Council](/wiki/National_Council_of_Slovenia "National Council of Slovenia"), the upper house of Slovenia's incompletely bicameral [Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_Slovenia "Parliament of Slovenia").[[18]](#cite_note-19)[[19]](#cite_note-20)

Dissolve the National Assembly and call snap elections
:   The president has [plenary authority](/wiki/Plenary_authority "Plenary authority") to [dissolve](/wiki/Dissolution_of_Parliament "Dissolution of Parliament") the National Assembly before the expiration of its term and call [snap elections](/wiki/Snap_election "Snap election"), but only if either of the following conditions are met:

1. The National Assembly fails to elect a prime minister within 30 days from the start of a new parliamentary session;[[20]](#cite_note-21) or
2. The National Assembly passes a [vote of no confidence](/wiki/Vote_of_no_confidence "Vote of no confidence") in the Government and fails to elect a new prime minister within 30 days.[[21]](#cite_note-22)

Express opinions to the National Assembly
:   The president of the Republic may, on their own initiative or whenever requested by the National Assembly, express an opinion on any matter of national import.[[22]](#cite_note-23)

Promulgate laws
:   The president of the Republic [promulgates](/wiki/Promulgation "Promulgation") each law passed by the National Assembly within eight days of its enactment, unless the law is sooner [vetoed](/wiki/Veto "Veto") by the National Council. The president does not have the discretion to refuse laws duly passed by the National Assembly and agreed to by the National Council.[[23]](#cite_note-24)

### Executive functions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=President_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Executive functions")]

Nominate the prime minister
:   The president of the Republic [nominates](/wiki/Government_formation "Government formation") for the consideration of the National Assembly a candidate for [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Slovenia "Prime Minister of Slovenia"), after consultation with the leaders of the various political parties represented in the National Assembly. The National Assembly in turn elects or rejects the president's nominee. If the National Assembly fails to elect a prime minister on the first round, the president may nominate one or more additional candidates for the consideration of the National Assembly in subsequent rounds, including a previously nominated candidate. This process continues until a prime minister is duly elected or the National Assembly is dissolved.[[24]](#cite_note-25)

Nominate and appoint certain other state officials
:   The president of the Republic nominates for the consideration of the National Assembly the governor of the [Central Bank of Slovenia](/wiki/Central_Bank_of_Slovenia "Central Bank of Slovenia"), the members of the [Court of Audit](/wiki/Court_of_Audit_of_the_Republic_of_Slovenia "Court of Audit of the Republic of Slovenia"), and the [Human Rights Ombudsman](/wiki/Ombudsman "Ombudsman").[[25]](#cite_note-26)[[26]](#cite_note-27)[[27]](#cite_note-28) The National Assembly in turn confirms or rejects the candidates so nominated. If confirmed, the nominees are formally appointed to office by the president.[[28]](#cite_note-29)

Represent Slovenia in foreign relations
:   The president of the Republic acts as Slovenia's supreme representative in international affairs.[[29]](#cite_note-30)[[30]](#cite_note-31) In this capacity, the president appoints and recalls Slovenian [ambassadors](/wiki/Ambassador "Ambassador") and [envoys](/wiki/Envoy_(title) "Envoy (title)"), [accepts](/wiki/Agrement "Agrement") the [credentials](/wiki/Letter_of_credence "Letter of credence") of foreign [diplomats](/wiki/Diplomatic_rank "Diplomatic rank") accredited to Slovenia, and both pays and receives [state visits](/wiki/State_visit "State visit").[[31]](#cite_note-32) Pursuant to Articles 14 and 59 of the Foreign Affairs Act, the president also decides on the opening and closure of [diplomatic missions](/wiki/Diplomatic_mission "Diplomatic mission"). In the performance of these duties, the president is required by law to consult the [foreign minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_Foreign_and_European_Affairs_(Slovenia) "Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs (Slovenia)") in order to ensure a coordinated and effective [foreign policy](/wiki/Foreign_relations_of_Slovenia "Foreign relations of Slovenia").[[b]](#cite_note-33) The president is nevertheless not obliged to follow any of the foreign minister's opinions, proposals, requests, or recommendations. Indeed, the president may act contrary to such advice.[[32]](#cite_note-34) In a similar vein, the president is empowered to issue instruments of ratification, whereby Slovenia expresses its consent as a [sovereign state](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state") to be bound by [treaty](/wiki/Treaty "Treaty") under [international law](/wiki/Vienna_Convention_on_the_Law_of_Treaties "Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties").[[33]](#cite_note-35) Despite this authority, the president's ability to refuse a treaty is limited. Treaty negotiation and conclusion is the responsibility of the Government, whereas [ratification](/wiki/Ratification "Ratification") itself rests exclusively with the National Assembly.[[34]](#cite_note-36)[[35]](#cite_note-37) Nevertheless, before affixing their instrument of ratification to any treaty, the president may request the Constitutional Court to opine on the treaty's [constitutionality](/wiki/Constitutionality "Constitutionality"). If the Constitutional Court concludes the treaty violates the Constitution, the treaty is deemed null and void. If the Constitutional Court finds the treaty is however in compliance with the Constitution, the president must issue an instrument of ratification.[[36]](#cite_note-38)

Serve as Commander-in-Chief
:   Supreme [command and control](/wiki/Command_and_control "Command and control") of the [Slovenian Armed Forces](/wiki/Slovenian_Armed_Forces "Slovenian Armed Forces") vests in the president of the Republic as [commander-in-chief](/wiki/Commander-in-chief "Commander-in-chief").[[37]](#cite_note-39) To this end, the president has plenary authority to declare a [state of emergency](/wiki/State_of_emergency "State of emergency") and to declare a [state of war](/wiki/State_of_war "State of war") if Slovenia is under attack. However, the president may exercise these broad powers only if the National Assembly is for whatever reason unable to convene.[[38]](#cite_note-40) Other duties have been conferred on the president by virtue of Slovenia's Defense Act. For example, the president:

1. [commissions](/wiki/Commission_(document) "Commission (document)") all [generals](/wiki/General "General"), [admirals](/wiki/Admiral "Admiral"), and other [commissioned officers](/wiki/Commissioned_officer "Commissioned officer") of the Armed Forces;
2. approves the annual guidelines prepared by the [defense minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defense_(Slovenia) "Ministry of Defense (Slovenia)") and submitted to the [chief of the General Staff](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Slovenia) "Chief of the General Staff (Slovenia)") detailing the operational strategy, materiel needs, and organizational plans of the Armed Forces;
3. prescribes the conditions and procedures for keeping him or herself informed about the [state of readiness](/wiki/Combat_readiness "Combat readiness") of the Armed Forces and other matters relevant to the [defence of the state](/wiki/National_security "National security");
4. receives regular briefings from the [Intelligence and Security Service](/wiki/Intelligence_and_Security_Service_of_the_Ministry_of_Defence_(Slovenia) "Intelligence and Security Service of the Ministry of Defence (Slovenia)");
5. adopts regulations with the force of law for the purpose of [administering](/wiki/Military_administration "Military administration") the activities of the Armed Forces, including matters of [military justice](/wiki/Military_justice "Military justice"), but only on the proposal of the Government;
6. orders the [mobilisation](/wiki/Mobilisation "Mobilisation") of the Armed Forces, but only on the proposal of the Government; and
7. decides upon the [use](/wiki/Combat "Combat") and [deployment](/wiki/Military_deployment "Military deployment") of the Armed Forces, the enforcement of [work](/wiki/Military_operations "Military operations") and [material duty](/wiki/Materiel "Materiel"), and (during a state of emergency) their total mobilisation.[[39]](#cite_note-41)

Issue decrees in times of national crisis
:   The president of the Republic may, at the request of the Government, issue [decrees](/wiki/Decree "Decree") with the force of law. Said decrees may, in exception, restrict individual [human rights](/wiki/Human_rights "Human rights") and [fundamental freedoms](/wiki/Civil_liberties "Civil liberties") otherwise guaranteed by Article 16 of the Constitution. The president's authority to make such decrees is only valid when the National Assembly is unable to convene and a state of war or emergency has been invoked.[[c]](#cite_note-43) The president may accept or refuse the Government's request.[[41]](#cite_note-44)

### Judicial functions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=President_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Judicial functions")]

Exercise the prerogative of clemency
:   The president of the Republic may, in their discretion, grant [general](/wiki/Amnesty "Amnesty") or [special](/wiki/Pardon "Pardon") pardons, [commutations](/wiki/Commutation_(law) "Commutation (law)"), [reprieves](/wiki/Pardon#Related_concepts "Pardon"), and [respites](/wiki/Pardon#Related_concepts "Pardon") to individuals convicted of criminal violations in Slovenian courts.[[42]](#cite_note-45) The president may choose to appoint a commission of experts to consult him or herself in the review of applications, but the prerogative of clemency is ultimately the president's alone.[[43]](#cite_note-46)

Nominate justices of the Constitutional Court
:   The president of the Republic nominates for the consideration of the National Assembly the nine justices of the [Constitutional Court](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_Slovenia "Constitutional Court of Slovenia"), who each serve staggered nonrenewable terms of nine years. The National Assembly in turn elects or rejects the candidates so nominated.[[44]](#cite_note-47)[[45]](#cite_note-48)[[46]](#cite_note-49)

Nominate members of the Judicial Council
:   The president of the Republic nominates for the consideration of the National Assembly five out of the eleven members of the Judicial Council.[[d]](#cite_note-52) Nominees must be university professors of law, senior counsel, or attorneys accepted to the State Bar. The National Assembly in turn elects or rejects the candidates so nominated.[[49]](#cite_note-53) Members of the Judicial Council serve staggered terms of six years upon confirmation, with either two or three of the presidentially nominated members subject to reelection every three years.[[50]](#cite_note-54)

Nominate Slovenian judges to international courts
:   The president of the Republic nominates for the consideration of the National Assembly Slovenia's judges on the [European Court of Justice](/wiki/European_Court_of_Justice "European Court of Justice"), the [European Court of Human Rights](/wiki/European_Court_of_Human_Rights "European Court of Human Rights"), and the [International Criminal Court](/wiki/International_Criminal_Court "International Criminal Court"). The National Assembly in turn elects or rejects the candidates so nominated.[[51]](#cite_note-55)

Confer and revoke state honours
:   The president of the Republic is Slovenia's [fount of honour](/wiki/Fount_of_honour "Fount of honour").[[52]](#cite_note-56) In this capacity, the president confers state honours on individuals for contributions to [civil society](/wiki/Civil_society "Civil society"), [diplomacy](/wiki/Diplomacy "Diplomacy") and [international affairs](/wiki/International_affairs "International affairs"), and [national security](/wiki/National_security "National security"). Pursuant to the Act on Decorations of the Republic of Slovenia, state honours take the form of various [medals](/wiki/Award "Award") and national [orders](/wiki/Order_(distinction) "Order (distinction)"), are conferred and revoked by decree, and are awarded at the president's discretion. Nevertheless, state authorities, local government authorities, organizations and citizens of the Republic of Slovenia may propose candidates for the president's consideration. Likewise, a Slovenian citizen cannot accept a foreign honour without the president's consent.[[53]](#cite_note-57)
